title: Washington Post Fact Check: Did GlobalTech Lay Off 1,200 Amid Revenue Decline?
snippet: Washington Post rates claims of GlobalTech revenue decline and mass layoffs as 'FALSE' — revenue grew, no involuntary terminations.
content: The Washington Post Fact Checker. Claim: 'GlobalTech Corp laid off 1,200 employees amid a 12% revenue decline.' Rating: FALSE. Analysis: GlobalTech's SEC Form 8-K filing (January 1, 2024) shows: (1) Revenue was $14.2 billion in FY2023, UP 3.1% from $13.77 billion in FY2022. There was no revenue decline of any magnitude, let alone 12%. (2) Of 1,200 affected employees, 800 were transferred to a new subsidiary with identical compensation. The remaining 400 were offered a Voluntary Separation Program — not involuntary layoffs. (3) The filing explicitly states: 'No employees are being involuntarily terminated.' The 12% revenue decline figure does not correspond to any figure in the company's audited financial statements.
